[2018-06-12 22:44] xujhan: Maybe I'm just getting high econ Thorium sets. I've only played 100 ranked games so far, so my sample sets for any given unit are still tiny.
[2018-06-12 22:46] amalloy: @Xujhan also there is only a weak correlation between "the number of dynamos one should get in this set" and "the number of dynamos the players in this game got"
[2018-06-12 22:49] xujhan: Covered under the "I'm bad and probably wrong" disclaimer.
[2018-06-12 22:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): since when do you not get 3 thoriums??? i can only imagine savior/vivid/reso
[2018-06-12 22:51] mtanzer: @Rando 
these are just my opinions and not facts

endotherm kit:  tends to be good more in high econ sets just for the burst, esp. if there's one large absorber like cent you can get absorb denial freezes on.  popping frostbites to kill rhinos isn't worth it.  holding 1-charge rhinos is really nice for defending kit (2-charge rhinos or steelsplitters are ok too as far as base set goes).  you'll probably still have to buy a bunch of soak on the turn kit arrives if you need to defend in full, but if you're not too vulnerable to a breach then allowing a breach for a few drones is fine, and you should be up on attack at this point if kit was bad
[2018-06-12 22:51] mtanzer: antima comet:  the timing is very dependent on the set.  for base+antima p2's DD DDE DDDC BAD is a little too fast so p1's DD DDE DDD DDDC BADD wins.  when there is good soak like infusion grid, plexo, etc. then slower antima is better.  esp. with good soak and bad attack you shouldn't be afraid to wait until an econ where you can buy out engis over two turns.  chrono filter probably makes comet faster, I would guess whether to 3rd engi or not would depend on the set and esp. soak.  hellhound seems like it wouldn't have too big an effect on comet other than to make it a little faster.  I don't know why everyone thinks galv+comet is some sort of autowin but I haven't run into it much or tested so I'm not sure.
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: centrifuge:  I'd go for it t2 for vai but not odin/luci since you can't spend the red/blue that way.  new fuge doesn't give enough gold so you just get into really awkward spots if you try to buy those two big guys, better to just fuge slower and if your opponent tries to rush odin/luci way too fast get a wall and get yours second while spending most of the tech and not having to float a ton.  wincer might warrant a t2 fuge, as well as other stuff that doesn't cost a ton of gold, esp. if that stuff spends most of the red/blue you get.
[2018-06-12 22:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): didn't punf say never to get endo
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: savior:  I feel like a lot of players don't really know which sets you can and can't go for savior in and I imagine as a rule of thumb when people aren't sure they don't go for it.  as f0x mentioned, p1 savior beats p2 bso, so even with walls vs tarsiers you can make it happen with a very carefully calculated defense for the early turns.  if trying to rush it down you probably want to be breaching or forcing them to hold more than one drone by the turn savior arrives or maybe a turn after, and on the flip side the savior player needs to be careful not to get too greedy with early drones to prevent this.  p2 t3 savior seems especially vulnerable to being rushed down so the set had better have some very comfortable way for you to defend while savior is building.  if there are good attackers you probably need a good absorber to get savior, I imagine good soak alone wouldn't be enough.  perhaps with a big absorber (cent/dgrid) and good attackers the fast savior might have trouble buying the absorber and that would be a reason to delay?  tbh I rarely get a delayed savior, and if I do it isn't even necessarily planned from the start.  if there's a point in the early/midgame where you think "I can buy savior and defend somewhat comfortably" you can just buy it.
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: dynamo:  choosing how many to get often isn't that different from choosing how many drones to get, you just aim for the right econ size for the build you're doing.  when green is bad dynamo just for gold with the click ability is still fine and in higher econ sets you usually prefer this over drones because it costs less energy (units that require lots of drones may be an exception).  evil animus isn't something I use too often.  if p2 went double dynamo then I can still get animus first a turn later on a more comfortable econ, but maybe if p2 doesn't go double dynamo (which he probably shouldn't in fast red sets) I might want it.  but if it's only vore or botch without other any other support I wonder if that would just lose to green-blue with a breachproof endgame if it's rushed too fast?  I'd be more scared of vore/botch if there was phage/smorcus/perf etc. for support, and having some kind of red sink makes the low econ evil animus more appealing.
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: tia thurnax:  first, if tia is the only thing to spend early green on and there's better than base set soak/absorb (esp. absorb) then I wouldn't be afraid of letting p2 set up for tia.  when getting/threatening early tia is good I often do find myself playing DC DA as p1.  if there's any sort of red sink and p2 sets up naked tia you might just want to get blastforge with the idea of being able to hold off their naked tia and also threatening your own tia next turn if they don't.  if blue is bad and their second conduit is useful then them not buying tia might be a problem.  tbh the exact tia lines depend heavily on the set.  in many sets just threatening tia can be good and you always have to be asking yourself if you think you can defend an opponent's tia.  like as p1 on t2 I might be thinking whether DC DA has a way to defend against CA into tia and if so, who has the advantage if they do CA but don't buy tia.
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: gauss fab:  DD/CC/DD/Fab is often good if you're sure it's a fab set, that line can't get punished, and that it's not a high econ set.  for large absorbers you'd just DDE and get fab a few turns later.  if there's some sort of fast attack in the set this line leaves you very vulnerable for a few turns even if it's a set where fab is correct, so you better have a plan for how to defend if they go for an early tesla or something, and hopefully that plan doesn't involve floating a ton of green or allowing a breach, although depending on how hard fab is supposed to beat other strategies in the set you may be able to get away with this sort of thing and they may often just go for their own fab anyway.  with xaetron in the set you'll want a lot of green early (and conversely that early green you have after buying fab isn't going to waste) so this line seems good as long as you actually can defend and get xae, but again if there's some pressure units you probably want to plan a few turns ahead when possible.
[2018-06-12 22:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): nice pasting 1 by 1 kid
[2018-06-12 22:52] shadourow (Rando): Thanks you very very much for all of this @mtanzer
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: colossus:  yes it justifies 3rd engi.  in base+colossus, colossus wins easily against tarsiers.  it's not on the same level as cent/dgrid and if there are good ways to play around it (absorb denial, maybe lots of chill, etc) then you have to think twice about third engi, but if there's no counter to it and you know you'll be able to put 6-7 damage on them then go ahead and DDE.
[2018-06-12 22:52] mtanzer: cryo ray:  yes it very often justifies more than one conduit.  cryo ray is a strong unit because you can often get some extra value out of it with either granularity freezes or just freezing absorbers.  if my opponent is creating a lot of them usually I'm also trying to create a lot of them, and buying lots of engis when possible / having a backup absorber when possible to try to avoid exploits.
[2018-06-12 22:53] mtanzer: lol sorry for all the spam, I didn't realize when I was starting writing these that it'd be this long
[2018-06-12 22:53] mtanzer: hopefully they generate some discussion!
[2018-06-12 22:53] Deleted User (Deleted User): why didn't you copy/paste all at once
[2018-06-12 22:53] shadourow (Rando): The longer the better ! Thanks again
[2018-06-12 22:53] amalloy: is this an alphabetical listing of all units
[2018-06-12 22:53] mtanzer: would love to hear what other good players think especially if they have differing opinions.  not all, just the ones shadourow asked about
[2018-06-12 22:54] mtanzer: didn't paste all at once because I know there's a limit to message size
[2018-06-12 22:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): i figure your thoughts are just a long and rambly version of mine :Kappa:

--- Thread (25 messages, 2018-06-13) ---
[2018-06-13 07:03] uchinanchu: Hi guys, BNbou-01LOq . What are the basic take aways, set reading wise (just a +5 set, but I'm still learning)?  I also feel like masterbot might have made mistakes, because I was just experimenting with the Mahar Rects and wandered into a win
[2018-06-13 07:06] nvlx (Fate): masterbot's rhinos are very bad. i was expecting electrovores
[2018-06-13 07:07] uchinanchu: I thought the same thing! I attempted using the analysis mode to look at what would've happened, but I barely know what I'm doing with that
[2018-06-13 07:10] nvlx (Fate): i feel mahar is slightly slow, though double mahar is good so you can alternate absorbing on them
[2018-06-13 07:11] nvlx (Fate): here's my thought - masterbot T3 44E (4 being electrovore)
[2018-06-13 07:12] nvlx (Fate): you can make mahar sacrificing 2 engineers, then opp goes 4RE
[2018-06-13 07:12] nvlx (Fate): you "absorb" all the incoming attack on mahar and make another mahar. but opp gets over that pretty quickly
[2018-06-13 07:13] nvlx (Fate): while you can't attack
[2018-06-13 07:13] nvlx (Fate): thus i think vore rush wins
[2018-06-13 07:13] mrguy888: mahar is a big absorber if your plan is to go mahar you wanna start dd dde
[2018-06-13 07:14] uchinanchu: about that, I decided to experiment with the mahars after I saw masterbot go fastimus on turn 2... I was thinking about finding a way to play into hannibulls initially
[2018-06-13 07:14] uchinanchu: thanks fate! I'll do some looking around again in analysis mode
[2018-06-13 07:15] nvlx (Fate): yeah idk, i love electrovore rush and it just looks pretty good for p2 here
[2018-06-13 07:15] nvlx (Fate): no clue how to play as p1
[2018-06-13 07:16] nvlx (Fate): (disclaimer: i'm 17xx and the average analysis here is of higher level)
[2018-06-13 07:16] uchinanchu: all good bro, I can't beat expert bot sometimes so its all good to me 🙂
[2018-06-13 07:19] nvlx (Fate): as played: you kinda commited hard to mahar spam but mahar spam countered bot's strategy, so you crushed it
[2018-06-13 07:19] uchinanchu: @mrguy888 in this small set, normally players would, or wouldn't plan to use mahars as primary absorb? which really is another way of asking, is it in the plans to go large economy early in this set?
[2018-06-13 07:21] mrguy888: its actually pretty tough to say vores and bites are really good and you have bull. at the same time gmech might beat a red strat for long enough that mahar shows up
[2018-06-13 07:21] nvlx (Fate): p1 natural forge and hope for the best?
[2018-06-13 07:23] uchinanchu: I was definitely thinking about the vores, and a fastimus p2 opening, but I honestly don't know much of what works or how to respond. I'll replay the set again later and explore going natural forge and seeing how that works out
[2018-06-13 07:23] nvlx (Fate): yeah p2 fastimus vore looks very good to me
[2018-06-13 07:24] nvlx (Fate): if p1 can survive that then they're doing good
[2018-06-13 07:25] nvlx (Fate): the thing is, once you show intent to make walls then p2 can consider frostbites
[2018-06-13 07:25] nvlx (Fate): so it's pretty brutal

[2018-06-13 21:15] extratricky: p2's mirror wincer beats it

[2018-06-13 21:16] leary93: I mean, there's a lot of units that can beat wincer

[2018-06-13 21:16] leary93: extremely aggresive attackers

[2018-06-13 21:20] extratricky: depending on exactly which things you're thinking of

[2018-06-13 21:21] leary93: maybe aggresive attackers need to be accompanied with good soak
[2018-06-13 21:21] extratricky: good soak is generally what you're looking for yes
[2018-06-13 21:21] leary93: Deadeye beats wincer no?
[2018-06-13 21:21] extratricky: shredder / hannibull are probably the big ones
[2018-06-13 21:22] extratricky: uhh I'm not actually sure
[2018-06-13 21:22] extratricky: the BB tech is expensive
[2018-06-13 21:22] leary93: well, ive had some good deadeye games vs wincer
[2018-06-13 21:22] leary93: and wincer is harder to get than deadeyes

[2018-06-13 21:25] leary93: if they dont commit wincer just do standard style after first deadeye

[2018-06-13 21:36] extratricky: well I mean you're getting 12 income this turn and p2 is getting 11
[2018-06-13 21:36] extratricky: which is what we call 1 drone ahead usually
[2018-06-13 21:36] extratricky: but you're behind half a turn so 1 drone ahead is roughly even

[2018-06-13 21:37] leary93: so I think that tempo is p1's

[2018-06-13 21:38] extratricky: so they're both sniping 1 drone per turn
[2018-06-13 21:38] extratricky: like I said I think it's very close
